role_title: Senior Associate, Innovation & New Bets - Strategy & Operations

level_signals: Expected to work independently with senior stakeholders without managing a team
Role spans strategy, operations, and direct selling — broad scope typical of mid-level IC

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You've done some version of consultative selling — whether that was your title or not
You have real product curiosity (you dig into how things work, not just how to position them)
You can build and test a pricing or GTM hypothesis, not just present one
You're fluent with AI tools (Claude Code, Cursor, similar) as part of your daily workflow
You can write clearly, present persuasively, and hold a room with senior stakeholders
You're comfortable spanning enterprise and SMB segments — different merchants, different motivations, different sales motions

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Restaurant or food tech operators who've been close to the merchant experience
SaaS go-to-market roles, especially at early-stage or growth-stage companies
Startup operators who've worn multiple hats and built processes from scratch
Consultants — but the kind who built things, not just advised on them

explicitly_excluded: Candidates who need structure handed to them — 'There's no established playbook, no onboarding curriculum for the products, no here's how we've always done it.'
Candidates more comfortable analyzing than doing — 'This role requires you to build things, sell things, and make decisions with incomplete information — not write recommendation decks.'
Candidates whose AI fluency is limited to using ChatGPT to wordsmith emails and polish slide decks
Candidates who operate better on an island — cross-functional collaboration is central
Candidates who want a clearly scoped lane — 'This is a Swiss-army-knife role across multiple products at different stages.'

scope.surface: DoorDash Commerce Platform — SaaS products for merchant partners (e.g. reputation management platform), serving enterprise restaurant brands, regional chains, and independent operators
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS / platform, serving restaurants, grocers, and retailers; DoorDash Drive fulfillment platform
primary_metrics_inferred: Product stickiness / retention across merchant segments
Revenue from merchant SaaS GTM motions
Sales conversion rates across enterprise, mid-market, and SMB segments
core_responsibilities: Iterate the sales playbook and product positioning across merchant segments
Run competitive positioning — map the landscape and build the case for merchant purchase
Sell directly to merchants via consultative sales motion
Design and run the merchant onboarding experience
Feed quantitative data and qualitative merchant feedback into the product roadmap
Work cross-functionally with Product, Engineering, Analytics, and Sales

green_flags: Compensation range explicitly disclosed ($87K-$128K national base)
Equity grants mentioned as part of compensation
Role is clearly scoped with concrete examples of responsibilities
Explicit AI tooling expectations (Claude Code, Cursor) signal a forward-looking team
Explicit about what the role is NOT — rare transparency reduces candidate mismatch
Cross-functional exposure across Product, Engineering, Analytics, and Sales
red_flags: Expected to be filled by 4/26/2026 — may already be closed or near closure at time of fetch (2026-05-10)
No explicit minimum years of experience stated — seniority level is ambiguous
Location type is unclear — JD mentions NYC-specific legal notices suggesting possible NYC base or remote, but does not clearly state remote eligibility or hybrid requirements
High ambiguity environment explicitly called out — could indicate organizational instability or under-resourced team
Role spans selling, onboarding, product feedback, and operations — risk of scope creep with unclear success metrics

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title 'Senior Associate' and comp range)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated in JD)
scope.business_model (inferred from product and platform description)
location.type (unclear — JD references NYC legal notices and 'Remote Jobs Associated With Office in NYC' but does not explicitly define hybrid vs remote policy)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred from 'Remote Jobs Associated With Office in NYC' language)
ambiguities: Location type is ambiguous: the JD includes a NYC-specific legal notice for 'NYC or Remote Jobs Associated With Office in NYC' but does not state whether this role is remote, hybrid, or onsite. Treated as 'unclear' with remote_us_eligible set to true as a conservative inference.
No explicit years-of-experience requirement is stated anywhere in the JD, making seniority calibration reliant solely on title and compensation.
The posting expected fill date is 4/26/2026, which is prior to the fetched_at date of 2026-05-10 — the role may no longer be active.
